name: Tutu Atwell
headline: Speedster joins Miami chasing a fragile target share
body:
Atwell has never converted his draft pedigree into stable production, and 2025 was a low point: a 27.9 percent snap share, just 15 targets, 6 catches for 192 yards, and a 2.6 percent target share over 8 games, ranking WR120. The lone bright spot was a 32.0 yards-per-reception, boom-or-bust profile built on a 20.13 air yards per target. That regressed hard from his 2024 line of 42 catches, 562 yards, and an 11.5 percent target share across 15 games.

Now in Miami after five years as a Rams role player, the 2021 second-rounder profiles as a vertical complement rather than a volume target, with a 40 percent catch rate in 2025 underscoring the inconsistency. Tyreek Hill and the Dolphins pecking order leave little room for a steady weekly role. He carries deep-league dart appeal on speed alone, but for redraft purposes he is a waiver-wire flier, not a draftable starter.
